offer.what: Full-service B2B print and marketing communications delivered through 270+ locally owned franchise centers. Categories: graphic design, digital/offset printing, large-format graphics and signage, vehicle wraps, direct mail, promotional products, packaging, and branding/identity services.
offer.coreOutcome: Businesses get high-quality, on-brand marketing materials and signage that help them attract customers, maintain brand consistency, and stand out — without needing an in-house design team or managing multiple vendors.
offer.pricePoint: Project-based custom quotes; no public pricing. Small orders (business cards, flyers) ~$50–$500; large-format/campaign work $1,000–$10,000+. No subscription — revenue is repeat-project-based. Corporate accounts may have standing purchase agreements.
offer.commitment: Per-project with no recurring contract; relationship-based repeat business is the norm for SMB and corporate accounts.
leadMagnets: Free quote / estimate (universal entry point via online form or in-person)
agEnterprise self-serve online ordering portal (for corporate/multi-location accounts)

audience.icp: SMB owner or marketing manager at a local/regional business (retail, professional services, real estate, restaurant, healthcare) needing reliable, high-quality print and marketing materials on a recurring basis. Secondary: corporate marketing/procurement managers at multi-location organizations.
painPoints: Inconsistent brand colors and quality across print vendors
Long or unpredictable turnaround times that miss campaign windows
Juggling multiple vendors for design, print, signage, and fulfillment
Online-only printers (Vistaprint, Canva Print) lack support, customization, and color accuracy
Difficulty scaling consistent branded materials across multiple locations
segments: Corporate / enterprise marketing and procurement teams
Franchisors needing centralized brand-consistent materials (National Programs)
Real estate professionals (high-volume repeat print buyers)

salesCycle.length: Days to 1–2 weeks (quote request → consultation → proof approval → production → delivery); same-day turnaround available for simple jobs
salesCycle.trial: Free quote/estimate is the universal entry point; free design consultation available at most locations
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Local franchise owner or sales associate; customer submits quote request (online form, phone, or in-person), receives proof and price, approves and pays, job enters production. Repeat customers often skip the formal quote stage.
salesCycle.topObjection: Price vs. commodity online printers. Overcome by emphasizing local support, faster turnaround, color accuracy, and ability to handle complex/custom work that online-only vendors cannot.

ahaMoment: Receiving the first finished product — business cards, banner, sign, or campaign kit — and seeing that quality and brand accuracy exceed what they could get online, delivered on time. Typically 1–5 business days from order placement; assisted by local franchise owner and design team.

retention.recurring: Not subscription-based but highly repeat-purchase. SMBs reorder on predictable cycles (business cards every 3–6 months, seasonal materials annually, event signage ad hoc). Corporate accounts may have standing portal-based orders.
retention.expansion: Customers who start with one category (e.g., business cards) are often unaware of the full range. Cross-sell to adjacent categories (signage, vehicle wraps, direct mail, promotional products) is the primary expansion lever.
retention.churnRisk: One-and-done customers who don't think of AlphaGraphics for their next need and default to cheaper online options. Also at risk: customers whose primary local contact changes, breaking the relationship.

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — no standard post-quote or post-first-order nurture sequence to orient new customers and introduce the full service range
Lead-Magnet / Quote Nurture — unconverted quote requests likely get a follow-up call but no email nurture sequence
Reorder Reminders — no triggered email when a time-based reorder window opens (e.g., 90 days after business card order)
New Product / Service Introduction — customers often unaware of categories beyond what they first ordered
Seasonal Promotions — trade show season, Q4 holiday, back-to-school windows not systematically messaged
Referral / Advocacy — no structured program to turn satisfied SMB customers into referral sources
Win-Back — dormant accounts (no order in 6+ months) receive no re-engagement sequence
toneVoice: Professional, confident, and locally warm. Speaks like a trusted neighborhood expert with big-brand capabilities — solution-focused, jargon-light, emphasizing speed, quality, and partnership. Not corporate-stiff; not overly casual. Peer-to-peer B2B register. Avoid discount-driven language; this is a quality/service brand.
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM / CASL. Franchise structure means list ownership may be split between corporate and individual location levels — list segmentation and permission management must be handled carefully per list source. No healthcare, financial, or legal content restrictions.
